title: papaver arenarium
intro: Papaver arenarium, also known as the gypsum poppy or gypsum poppy meadow poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is often found growing in dry, stony areas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as the seeds contain alkaloids that have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating pain and inflammation. The seeds and roots of the plant can be made into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to relieve respiratory issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations.
